---
title: "What I've been finding: my iNaturalist sightings"
subtitle: "A naturalist's logbook, kept up to date straight from the iNaturalist API"
description: >
  Favourite sightings, the rare and threatened ones, and what the numbers say
  about where and what I observe — pulled live from iNaturalist and refreshed
  automatically.
date: 2026-06-26
categories: [nature, data, south africa, api]
image: inat-by-group.png   # produced on render
image-alt: "Observations by taxonomic group"
freeze: false              # always re-run: the data file updates out of band
draft: true                # <- remove to publish
---

```{python}
#| label: load
#| echo: false
import json, datetime
data = json.load(open("inat.json", encoding="utf-8"))
s = data["stats"]
gen = data["generated_at"][:10]
```

Every walk on the mountain or the shoreline ends the same way: phone out,
photographing something I can't name yet. [iNaturalist](https://www.inaturalist.org)
turns that habit into data — and since the numbers below come from its API and
refresh on their own, this page is never out of date.

```{python}
#| label: stat-cards
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import HTML
def card(value, label):
    return (f'<div style="flex:1;min-width:120px;border:1px solid #e2e2e2;'
            f'border-radius:10px;padding:.9rem 1rem;text-align:center">'
            f'<div style="font-size:1.6rem;font-weight:700;color:#0b5e54">{value}</div>'
            f'<div style="font-size:.8rem;color:#666">{label}</div></div>')
pct = round(100 * s["research_grade"] / max(s["observations"], 1))
HTML('<div style="display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;margin:1rem 0">' + "".join([
    card(f'{s["observations"]:,}', "observations"),
    card(f'{s["species"]:,}', "species"),
    card(f'{s["threatened_observations"]:,}', "threatened taxa"),
    card(f'{pct}%', "research grade"),
]) + '</div>')
```

## Favourite sightings

A few I keep coming back to. Conservation status, where iNaturalist records it,
is flagged.

```{python}
#| label: gallery
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import HTML
TEAL = "#0b5e54"
cards = []
for f in data["favourites"]:
    cs = f.get("conservation_status")
    badge = (f'<span style="background:{TEAL};color:#fff;border-radius:4px;'
             f'padding:1px 6px;font-size:.72rem">{cs}</span>') if cs else ""
    name = f.get("common_name") or f.get("species") or "Unknown"
    sci = f.get("species") or ""
    place = f.get("place") or ""
    img = f.get("photo") or ""
    cards.append(f'''
      <figure style="margin:0">
        <a href="{f['uri']}"><img src="{img}" loading="lazy" alt="{name}"
           style="width:100%;aspect-ratio:1;object-fit:cover;border-radius:8px"></a>
        <figcaption style="font-size:.82rem;line-height:1.3;margin-top:.35rem">
          <strong>{name}</strong> {badge}<br>
          <em style="color:#666">{sci}</em><br>
          <span style="color:#888">{place} · ♥ {f.get('faves',0)}</span>
        </figcaption>
      </figure>''')
HTML('<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(180px,1fr));'
     'gap:1rem">' + "".join(cards) + '</div>')
```

## By the numbers

```{python}
#| label: fig-by-group
#| fig-cap: "Observations by taxonomic group."
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
g = s["by_group"]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3.4))
ax.barh(list(g)[::-1], list(g.values())[::-1], color="#0b5e54")
ax.set_xlabel("observations")
fig.tight_layout(); fig.savefig("inat-by-group.png", dpi=130)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-by-year
#| fig-cap: "Observations per year — the shape of a growing life list."
#| echo: false
y = s["by_year"]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 2.8))
ax.bar(list(y), list(y.values()), color="#0b5e54")
ax.set_ylabel("observations"); plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
fig.tight_layout()
```

### Most-observed species

```{python}
#| label: tbl-top
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame(s["top_species"])
df.index = range(1, len(df) + 1)
df.columns = ["species", "common name", "times observed"]
df
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## How this stays current
A scheduled GitHub Action runs `scripts/fetch_inaturalist.py` (public
iNaturalist API, no credentials), writes `inat.json`, commits it, and re-renders
the site. The page reads only that file, so rendering is fast and offline; the
freshness comes from the schedule. Locations for threatened taxa are obscured by
iNaturalist, so nothing here pinpoints a sensitive species.

```{python}
#| label: refreshed
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
print(f"*Last refreshed: {gen}. Photos and observations © the observer, via iNaturalist.*")
```
:::
